notes.note: The senders joining Cicero in the greeting are his brother Quintus ("Q. pater," Quintus the father) and Quintus's son, Cicero's nephew ("filius"); young Marcus, Cicero's own son, is the "Cicero" already named.

notes.anchor: because Quintus had not caught up with us
notes.note: Quintus is Cicero's younger brother, who had served as his legate in Cilicia and was travelling home in the same party.
Tiro was Cicero's slave before being freed (probably in 53 BC); Cicero playfully styles himself "magister," the affectionate patron-tutor relation that survived the manumission.

notes.anchor: the eighth day before the Ides of November
notes.note: A Roman date counted inclusively back from the Ides (the 13th in November), giving 6 November 50 BC.
